gardening_summary: Best planting month of the year. Cool and dry — ideal for most vegetables.
Still excellent. Slightly warming but most winter crops still productive.
Warming up fast. Last chance for cool crops. Start heat-tolerant ones.
Getting hot. Transition month. Only heat-tolerant crops now.
Very hot and dry. Challenging for most crops. Focus on heat-hardy only.
Hottest dry period. Very few crops suitable. Protect existing plants from sun.
Monsoon arrives. High humidity. Fungal disease risk rises. Reduce watering.
Peak monsoon. Waterlogging risk. Focus on drainage. Humidity-tolerant crops only.
Monsoon ends. Humidity still high but decreasing. Transition to winter prep.
Great transition month. Temperature dropping. Prepare beds for winter season.
Excellent month. Cool season begins. Best time to start winter vegetable garden.
Peak cool season. Best conditions for widest variety of vegetables.
